questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: Some politicians are honest. No honest person is corrupt. All corrupt people are selfish. Conclusions: I. Some politicians are not selfish. II. Some selfish people are not honest.

questions.stem: The organisation ____ to improve its bottom line.

questions.stem: Synonym of OBSTINATE:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Neither the manager nor his assistants was present.
options: Neither the manager nor his assistants are being present.
Neither the manager nor his assistants has been present.
Neither the manager nor his assistants were present.
Neither the manager nor his assistants is present.
questions.solution: Neither the manager nor his assistants were present.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of ELOQUENT:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is suffering with high fever.
options: He is suffering from high fever.
He is suffering of high fever.
He is suffering by high fever.
He is suffering with high fever.
questions.solution: He is suffering from high fever.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of DISPARITY:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The frequency division by a T flip-flop is:
questions.solution: T FF toggles each clock, so output frequency = input/2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Symbol table stores:
options: Information about identifiers (name, type, scope, memory location)
questions.solution: Symbol table: maps identifier to attributes (type, scope, offset, etc.).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Min-cut / Max-flow theorem:
options: The maximum flow value equals the minimum capacity of a cut (dual problem)
questions.solution: Min-cut = partition separating source and sink with minimum capacity. Max-flow = min-cut capacity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A subquery in SQL is:
options: A query nested inside another query
questions.solution: Subquery (inner query) = nested SELECT inside outer SELECT/FROM/WHERE.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The SR latch has the invalid state when:
questions.solution: SR latch: S=1,R=1 → Q=Q'=1 (invalid for NOR-based).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Syntax analysis uses:
options: Context-free grammar (CFG) to build a parse tree
questions.solution: Parser: CFG → parse tree or AST (syntax tree).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The worst-case time complexity of mergesort for n=16 is:
questions.solution: Mergesort always O(n log n). For n=16: O(16 log 16).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ACID properties stand for:
questions.solution: ACID: A=all or nothing, C=valid state, I=concurrent invisible, D=persistent after commit.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A half adder has:
questions.solution: Half adder: Sum = A⊕B, Carry = A·B.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The symbol table is implemented using:
options: Hash tables or binary search trees for efficient lookup
questions.solution: Symbol table = hash table (O(1)) or BST (O(log n)) for identifier lookup.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: T(n) = 2T(n/4) + √n solves to:
questions.solution: Master theorem: a=2, b=4, f(n)=n^(1/2). Compare: n^(log₄2) = √n. Case 2: O(√n log n).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BASE stands for:
options: Basically Available, Soft state, Eventually consistent (NoSQL)
questions.solution: BASE = NoSQL trade-off (eventual consistency). Opposite of ACID.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A ring counter with n FFs has:
questions.solution: Ring: n states (shift register with feedback). Johnson: 2n states.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reference counting GC:
options: Counts references to each object; deallocates when count reaches 0
questions.solution: Reference counting: simple but can't handle circular references. Often combined with tracing GC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The edit distance problem measures:
options: Minimum operations (insert, delete, substitute) to convert one string to another
questions.solution: Edit distance = DP for string transformation cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A superkey is:
options: A set of attributes that uniquely identifies a row (superset of candidate key)
questions.solution: Superkey: any superset of a candidate key (may contain redundant attributes).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A decoder converts:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shift-reduce parser uses:
options: A stack and a parsing table
A queue and a symbol table
questions.solution: Shift-reduce: shift (push token) or reduce (pop RHS, push LHS).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dynamic programming bottom-up solves problems by:
options: Solving smaller subproblems first and storing results
questions.solution: DP: tabulation (bottom-up) or memoization (top-down with caching).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A materialized view:
options: Stores the query result physically (refreshed periodically or on demand)
questions.solution: Materialized view: trade-off (fast reads, stale data). Used in data warehouses for aggregate queries.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The 2's complement of binary 1010 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lexical analysis produces:
questions.solution: Lexer: input chars → tokens (e.g., IDENTIFIER, KEYWORD, NUMBER).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dijkstra's algorithm finds shortest paths correctly only when edge weights are:
questions.solution: Dijkstra assumes non-negative weights; negative edges require Bellman–Ford.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The SQL MERGE statement:
options: Performs INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE operations based on source data (upsert)
questions.solution: MERGE: also called UPSERT. Matched → UPDATE, not matched → INSERT. SQL Server, Oracle, PostgreSQL.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Setup time is:
options: The minimum time data must be stable before the clock edge
questions.solution: Setup time: data must be stable before clock (violation → metastability).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Code optimization aims to:
options: Improve execution speed or reduce code size
questions.solution: Optimization: constant folding, dead code elimination, loop unrolling, etc.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Binary search on a sorted array of 16 elements needs at most how many comparisons?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The ARIES recovery algorithm:
options: Uses WAL, redo all committed, undo uncommitted (three passes: analysis, redo, undo)
questions.solution: ARIES (Algorithms for Recovery and Isolation Exploiting Semantics): steal/no-force + WAL + Fuzzy Checkpointing.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The JK flip-flop eliminates the invalid state by:
questions.solution: JK FF: J=1,K=1 → toggle (Q ← Q'). No invalid state.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Constant propagation replaces:
options: Variables whose values are known constants at compile time
questions.solution: Constant propagation: x = 5; y = x + 3 → y = 8 (compile-time evaluation).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The time complexity of binary search for n=64 is:
questions.solution: Binary search halves the search space each step: O(log n) ≈ O(log 64).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The entity-relationship (ER) model represents:
questions.solution: ER model = conceptual design: entities (rectangles), attributes (ovals), relationships (diamonds).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The state diagram of a Mealy machine has:
questions.solution: Mealy: output = f(input, current state). Moore: output = f(current state only).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The lexer uses:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The number of comparisons in merge sort is:
questions.solution: Mergesort: C(n) = 2C(n/2) + n → n log n comparisons.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A join in SQL:
options: Combines rows from two or more tables based on a related column
questions.solution: JOIN types: INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL OUTER, CROSS, NATURAL.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An up/down counter uses:
options: A control input to select count direction
questions.solution: Up/down counter: control input (U/D) determines direction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Register allocation is typically done by:
questions.solution: Register allocation: colors interference graph with k colors (k registers).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The maximum number of comparisons made by bubble sort on 8 elements is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The isolation level SERIALIZABLE:
options: Ensures complete isolation (transactions execute as if serial)
questions.solution: SERIALIZABLE = highest isolation (no concurrency anomalies). Lowest = READ UNCOMMITTED.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The XOR of A and B is 1 when:
questions.solution: XOR: A ⊕ B = A'B + AB'. Output 1 when inputs differ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Software pipelining:
options: Reorganizes loop iterations to overlap execution of different iterations
questions.solution: Software pipelining: schedules instructions from different iterations to fill pipeline slots.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Breadth-first search (BFS) uses:
questions.solution: BFS = queue (level order). DFS = stack (or recursion).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The 'D' in ACID (Durability) means:
options: Committed changes persist even after system failures
questions.solution: Durability: once committed, changes are permanent (survives crashes).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The number of rows in the truth table for n inputs is:
questions.solution: Truth table has 2^n rows (all possible input combinations).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Garbage collection:
options: Automatic memory reclamation (marks reachable objects, collects unreachable)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Tower of Hanoi recurrence T(n) = 2T(n-1) + 1 solves to:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The 'I' in ACID (Isolation) means:
options: Concurrent transactions don't interfere with each other
questions.solution: Isolation: each transaction appears to execute in isolation (serializable).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the NAND gate shown with inputs A = 1 and B = 0, the output is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 110" width="220" height="110"><line x1="10" y1="35" x2="70" y2="35" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="75" x2="70" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="6" y="30" font-size="11" fill="#2563eb">A=1</text><text x="6" y="92" font-size="11" fill="#2563eb">B=0</text><path d="M70 20 L110 20 Q150 55 110 90 L70 90 Z" fill="#f1f5f9" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="95" y="60" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">NAND</text><line x1="150" y1="55" x2="200" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><text x="180" y="48" font-size="11" fill="#dc2626">?</text></svg>
figure.caption: NAND gate

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Loop unrolling:
options: Replicates loop body multiple times to reduce loop overhead
questions.solution: Loop unrolling: trade-off (code size ↑, loop overhead ↓). Factor = unroll count.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Johnson's algorithm for all-pairs shortest path uses:
questions.solution: Johnson: reweight edges with Bellman-Ford, then Dijkstra from each vertex. O(V² log V + VE).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A covering index:
options: Contains all columns needed by a query (no need to access table)
questions.solution: Covering index = query can be answered entirely from index (index-only scan).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An SR flip-flop has the invalid state when:
questions.solution: SR FF: S=1,R=1 is forbidden (both outputs become 0 for NOR-based).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A parse tree is:
options: A graphical representation of a derivation
questions.solution: Parse tree: root = start symbol, leaves = terminals, internal nodes = non-terminals.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Articulation points (cut vertices):
options: Vertices whose removal disconnects the graph (found via DFS low-link values)
questions.solution: Tarjan's algorithm: DFS + discovery time + low value. Root is articulation point if it has ≥2 children.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A foreign key is:
options: A key in the same table
An attribute referencing a primary key in another table (maintains referential integrity)
questions.solution: Foreign key: ensures referential integrity (child table references parent).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: De Morgan's law states (A·B)' = :

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A B-tree of order m has a maximum of ____ children per node:
questions.solution: B-tree order m: max m children, min ⌈m/2⌉ children (except root).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The memory hierarchy (fastest to slowest) is:
questions.solution: Fastest: registers, L1/L2/L3 cache, RAM, SSD, HDD (slowest).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3-to-8 decoder has:
questions.solution: n-to-2^n decoder: n inputs, 2^n outputs (only one output active at a time).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The well-known TCP port for unencrypted HTTP is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 200×80 array of 8-byte elements occupies how much memory?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In 2's complement representation, the range for 8-bit signed numbers is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Harvard architecture uses:
options: Separate memory for instructions and data
questions.solution: Harvard = separate instruction and data memories/buses.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CISC stands for:
questions.solution: CISC: complex instructions, variable length, memory operands allowed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Normalization 2NF requires:
options: 1NF + no partial dependency (non-key attributes depend on all of the primary key)
questions.solution: 2NF: remove partial dependencies (applicable to composite keys).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal value of the hexadecimal number A₁₆ is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: External sorting is used when:
options: Data doesn't fit in main memory (uses disk I/O)
questions.solution: External sort: merge sort variant (create sorted runs, merge using k-way merge).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: TLB access = 20 ns, memory access = 100 ns, TLB hit ratio = 0.95 (paged memory). The effective access time is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Von Neumann architecture uses:
options: A single shared memory for data and instructions
Separate memory for data and instructions
questions.solution: Von Neumann = single bus for data and instructions. Harvard = separate buses.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Interrupt-driven I/O:
options: Device signals CPU when ready (efficient, CPU can do other work)
questions.solution: Interrupt: device asserts interrupt line → CPU saves context → runs ISR → restores.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Three Cs of cache misses:
options: Compulsory (first access), Capacity (working set > cache), Conflict (mapping collisions)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The memory hierarchy (fastest to slowest):
options: Register → L1 → L2 → L3 → RAM → SSD → HDD
questions.solution: Hierarchy by speed: registers < 1 ns, L1 ~1 ns, L2 ~4 ns, L3 ~10 ns, RAM ~100 ns, SSD ~10 μs, HDD ~10 ms.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Endianness refers to:
options: Byte ordering (big-endian: MSB first; little-endian: LSB first)
questions.solution: Big-endian (network byte order): most significant byte at lowest address. Little: x86, most ARM.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The write-back policy writes to memory:
options: Only when the cache line is evicted (dirty line)
questions.solution: Write-back: only main memory on eviction (with dirty bit). Write-through: immediately.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The clock cycle time is the reciprocal of:
questions.solution: Cycle time = 1/frequency. 3 GHz CPU = ~0.33 ns per cycle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Linux uses which type of kernel?
questions.solution: Linux: monolithic + loadable kernel modules (LKM) for dynamic loading.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 3, push 8, push 20, pop, push 21. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 20 then pop (removes 20), pushing 21 leaves 21 on top (LIFO).

figure.caption: Stack state

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: QoS (Quality of Service) in networking:
options: Prioritizes certain traffic types (voice, video) over others
questions.solution: QoS: DiffServ (DSCP marking), CoS (802.1p priority). Traffic policing, shaping, queuing (WFQ, LLQ).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A perfect binary tree of height 4 (root at height 1) has how many nodes?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An AVL tree with 7 nodes has a minimum height of:
questions.solution: AVL: height ≤ 1.44 log₂ n. For n=7, min height = 3 (log₂ 8).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 3, push 11, push 12, pop, push 21. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 12 then pop (removes 12), pushing 21 leaves 21 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Operations on an empty stack: push 3, push 9, push 12, pop, push 25. The element now on top is:
questions.solution: After push 12 then pop (removes 12), pushing 25 leaves 25 on top (LIFO).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With a page size of 2 KB, the number of bits used for the page offset is:
questions.solution: 2KB = 2^11 bytes, so the offset field is 11 bits.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The longest increasing subsequence (LIS) can be solved in:
options: O(n log n) using patience sorting (binary search on tails)
questions.solution: LIS: maintain tails[i] = min possible tail of increasing subsequence of length i. Binary search each element.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal value of the binary number 101₂ is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A demultiplexer with n select lines has:
questions.solution: DEMUX: 1 input routed to one of 2^n outputs based on select lines.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A Turing machine has:
options: An infinite tape and a read/write head
questions.solution: Turing machine = infinite tape + R/W head + finite control.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The maximum number of comparisons made by bubble sort on 5 elements is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The PCB (Process Control Block) contains:
options: Process state, registers, priority, memory info, open files
questions.solution: PCB = kernel data structure for each process (task_struct in Linux).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The size of virtual address space is limited by:
options: Number of bits in the address (e.g., 2^64)
questions.solution: Virtual address space = 2^(address bits), independent of RAM.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: C++20 Modules:
options: Replace header files with a more efficient compilation model (import/export)
questions.solution: Modules: export module foo; import <iostream>;. Faster compilation, better encapsulation than headers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Every recursive function must have a:
questions.solution: Without a base (terminating) case, recursion never stops and overflows the stack.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quick sort worst-case occurs when:
options: The pivot is always the minimum or maximum element
questions.solution: Worst case: pivot always min or max = O(n²). Sorted array with first element as pivot = O(n²).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sum of minterms expression is also called:
questions.solution: SOP: OR of AND terms (minterms). POS: AND of OR terms (maxterms).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The number of edges in a complete graph with n nodes is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A constructor in C++ is:
options: A function that destroys the object
A special member function called when an object is created
questions.solution: Constructor initializes objects. Destructor cleans up when object is destroyed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The SQL clause used to retrieve data from a table is:
questions.solution: SELECT … FROM … queries rows from one or more tables.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Technical debt refers to:
options: The future cost of quick-and-dirty software development choices
questions.solution: Technical debt = shortcuts that slow future development (needs refactoring).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The congestion avoidance phase increases cwnd by:
questions.solution: Congestion avoidance: additive increase (cwnd += 1/cwnd per ACK).
